user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to browse the remote connector catalog with filters so that I can discover connectors available to install.
expected_behavior: Queries connector_registry_cache with optional category, search (ILIKE over name/description/connector_type), tags (comma-split, matched with the && array-overlap operator), and verified_only filters; supports limit (default 50) and offset (default 0) pagination. Returns 200 {success:true, data:[rows], pagination:{total,count,limit,offset}} where total comes from a COUNT(*) variant of the same query. On error returns 500 with success:false.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view a single connector's catalog entry so that I can review its metadata before installing.
expected_behavior: Selects the row from connector_registry_cache where connector_type = :type. Returns 200 {success:true, data:row} when found, or 404 {success:false, error:'Not Found', message:"Connector '<type>' not found in registry"} when absent. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to search the connector catalog by keyword so that I can quickly find a relevant connector.
expected_behavior: Intended to query connector_registry_cache by ILIKE over name/description/connector_type plus tag ANY-match, ranked (exact type match first, then name), limited by q and limit (default 20), returning {success:true, data, count, query}. In practice this handler is never reached: the route GET /registry/:type is registered before GET /registry/search, so Express binds /registry/search to getRegistryDetails with type='search', which queries connector_type='search' and returns 404 'Connector \'search\' not found in registry'.
issue_note: GET /registry/search is shadowed by the earlier GET /registry/:type route; searchRegistry is unreachable (connector.routes.ts:57 registered before :63).

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to list installed connectors so that I can see what is available for configuration.
expected_behavior: Queries installed_connectors with optional category, enabled (string compared to 'true'), and search (ILIKE) filters. sort_by/sort_order are passed through validateConnectorSortField/validateSortDirection before being interpolated, guarding against SQL injection. Returns 200 {success:true, data:[rows], count}. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view an installed connector's details so that I can inspect its version, capabilities, and resources.
expected_behavior: Selects the row from installed_connectors where connector_type = :type. Returns 200 {success:true, data:row} when found, or 404 'Connector \'<type>\' is not installed' when absent. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to install a connector from the registry so that I can begin configuring it.
expected_behavior: Body validated by Joi (connector_type required, optional version, force). Returns 409 Conflict if already installed and force is not true; 404 if connector_type is absent from connector_registry_cache. Otherwise upserts a row into installed_connectors (ON CONFLICT(connector_type) DO UPDATE) copying category/name/description/capabilities/resources/configuration_schema/tags from the catalog entry, defaults version to catalog latest_version, and returns 201 {success:true, data:row, message}. Note: actual package download/extraction/dependency install is a documented TODO stub; install_path is set to /opt/happycmdb/connectors/<type> without verifying any files exist.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to update an installed connector to a newer version so that I get the latest fixes and features.
expected_behavior: Returns 404 if the connector is not installed; 400 if no version is supplied and the registry has no latest_version. If the target equals the current version and force is not set, returns 200 with up_to_date:true. Otherwise UPDATEs installed_connectors.installed_version/latest_available_version/updated_at and returns 200 {success:true, data:row, previous_version, new_version, message}. Actual download/backup/migration is a TODO stub; only the DB record is changed.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to uninstall a connector so that I can remove integrations I no longer need.
expected_behavior: Returns 404 if the connector is not installed. Guards against orphaning configs: counts connector_configurations for the type and returns 400 if any exist ('Delete configurations first'). Otherwise DELETEs the installed_connectors row and returns 200 {success:true, message}. File/dependency cleanup is a TODO stub; only the DB record is removed.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to verify a connector's installation so that I can trust it is correctly installed before configuring it.
expected_behavior: Returns 404 if the connector is not installed. Otherwise it is meant to check installation files, dependencies, connector.json, and loadability. In the current code verification is a hardcoded stub: `const verified = true`, which is written to installed_connectors.verified and returned as {success:true, verified:true, message:'...passed'} for every installed connector regardless of actual state.
issue_note: verifyConnector unconditionally sets verified=true (connector.controller.ts:572), so verification always reports success and gives false confidence.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to refresh the connector registry cache so that the catalog reflects the latest published connectors.
expected_behavior: Fetches the remote catalog JSON from CONNECTOR_REGISTRY_URL (default the happycmdb GitHub catalog.json) with a 10s axios timeout. Throws/500s if the payload lacks a connectors array. On success DELETEs all connector_registry_cache rows then re-inserts each catalog connector (versions JSON-stringified, cache_expires_at = NOW()+24h) and returns 200 {success:true, count, updated_at}. Side effect: full cache replacement (not atomic/transactional).

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to see which installed connectors have newer versions so that I can plan updates.
expected_behavior: LEFT JOINs installed_connectors against connector_registry_cache and selects rows where installed_version != latest_version OR the catalog entry is missing. Returns 200 {success:true, data:[{connector_type,name,installed_version,available_version,updated_at}], count, message} where the message reflects whether updates are available. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to list connector configurations so that I can manage all configured integrations.
expected_behavior: Delegates to ConnectorConfigCRUDController.listConfigurations, which builds a query via buildListQuery with optional connector_type, enabled, schedule_enabled, search, sort_by/sort_order, and limit (default 100)/offset (default 0). Returns 200 {success:true, data:[rows], pagination:{total,count,limit,offset}} where total comes from the count query. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view a connector configuration's details so that I can review its connection and resource settings.
expected_behavior: Selects from connector_configurations where id = :id. Returns 200 {success:true, data:row} when found, or 404 "Configuration with ID '<id>' not found" when absent. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to create a connector configuration so that I can define how a connector connects and what it syncs.
expected_behavior: Joi-validates the body, then validateConfiguration enforces name, connector_type, and connection (returns 400 if missing). Returns 400 if the connector_type is not present in installed_connectors, and 409 if a configuration with the same name already exists. Otherwise INSERTs into connector_configurations with sensible defaults (enabled=true, schedule_enabled=false, max_retries=3, retry_delay_seconds=300, continue_on_error=false, notification_on_failure=true), JSON-stringifying connection/options/resource_configs, and returns 201 {success:true, data:row, message}. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to update a connector configuration so that I can change its connection details, schedule, or resources.
expected_behavior: Returns 400 if id is missing, 404 if the configuration does not exist. buildUpdateQuery restricts updates to an allowlist of fields (name, description, enabled, schedule, schedule_enabled, connection, options, enabled_resources, resource_configs, max_retries, retry_delay_seconds, continue_on_error, notification_*), JSON-stringifying connection/options/resource_configs and always bumping updated_at; returns 400 'No valid fields to update' if none match. On success UPDATEs and returns 200 {success:true, data:row}. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to delete a connector configuration so that I can remove integrations I no longer use.
expected_behavior: Returns 404 if the configuration does not exist; otherwise DELETEs the connector_configurations row and returns 200 {success:true, message:'Configuration deleted successfully'}. No cascade handling of run history is performed in this handler. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to test a configuration's connection so that I can confirm credentials and connectivity before enabling it.
expected_behavior: Returns 404 if the configuration does not exist. Otherwise it is meant to perform a live connection test against the connector. In the current code the test is a stub that unconditionally returns 200 {success:true, message:'Connection test passed', details:{connector_type, tested_at}} for any existing configuration without contacting the target system.
issue_note: testConnection always returns success without performing a real connection test (operations.controller.ts:100-110), giving false confidence in connectivity.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to trigger a manual connector run so that I can sync data on demand.
expected_behavior: Optional body (resource_id, triggered_by default 'manual'). Returns 404 if the configuration does not exist and 400 if it is disabled. Otherwise INSERTs a connector_run_history row with status 'queued' and returns 202 {success:true, data:run, message:'Connector run queued successfully'}. However the actual job enqueue (BullMQ) is a TODO stub, so the run is recorded as 'queued' but is never picked up or executed.
issue_note: runConnector inserts a 'queued' run record but never enqueues a BullMQ job (operations.controller.ts:151), so manual runs never execute.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to enable a configuration so that scheduled and manual runs are allowed.
expected_behavior: UPDATEs connector_configurations SET enabled=true, updated_at=NOW() for :id with RETURNING. Returns 404 if no row matched, otherwise 200 {success:true, data:row, message:'Configuration enabled successfully'}. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to disable a configuration so that it stops running while I troubleshoot or pause an integration.
expected_behavior: UPDATEs connector_configurations SET enabled=false, updated_at=NOW() for :id with RETURNING. Returns 404 if no row matched, otherwise 200 {success:true, data:row, message:'Configuration disabled successfully'}. A disabled config causes /run to return 400. Errors return 500.

feature: List available resources for a configuration
user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to see which resources a configuration's connector supports so that I can choose what to sync.
expected_behavior: JOINs connector_configurations to installed_connectors on connector_type to pull the connector's declared resources/metadata. Returns 404 if the config (or its joined connector) is not found, otherwise 200 {success:true, data:{config_id, connector_type, available_resources, enabled_resources}} where available_resources come from the installed connector and enabled_resources from the config. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to set which resources are enabled and their per-resource config so that I control exactly what the connector syncs.
expected_behavior: Joi-validated body (enabled_resources, resource_configs). UPDATEs connector_configurations SET enabled_resources, resource_configs (JSON-stringified), updated_at=NOW() with RETURNING. Returns 404 if no row matched, otherwise 200 {success:true, data:row, message}. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view the configuration for a single resource so that I can inspect its sync options.
expected_behavior: Selects resource_configs from connector_configurations for :id. Returns 404 if the config does not exist, otherwise 200 {success:true, data:{config_id, resource_id, config}} where config is resource_configs[resourceId] or {} when that key is absent. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view the run history for a configuration so that I can audit its past executions.
expected_behavior: Delegates to metrics controller; builds a query via buildRunsQuery scoped to config_id=:id with optional resource_id and status filters, limit (default 100)/offset (default 0), sort_by (default started_at)/sort_order (default desc). Returns 200 {success:true, data:[runs], pagination:{total,count,limit,offset}}. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want aggregate metrics for a configuration so that I can assess its reliability and throughput.
expected_behavior: Aggregates connector_run_history for config_id=:id: total_runs, successful_runs (status='completed'), failed_runs (status='failed'), avg_duration_ms, total_records_extracted, total_records_loaded, and a derived success_rate percentage (0 when no runs). Returns 200 {success:true, data:{...}}. Always 200 even when the config has no runs (zeros). Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want the latest metrics for a specific resource so that I can monitor per-resource sync health.
expected_behavior: Selects the most recent row from connector_resource_metrics where config_id=:id AND resource_id=:resourceId (ORDER BY measured_at DESC LIMIT 1). Returns 200 {success:true, data:row|null} (null when no metrics exist; does not 404). Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view all connector runs across configurations so that I can monitor activity system-wide.
expected_behavior: Builds a query via buildRunsQuery with optional config_id, connector_type, resource_id, and status filters, limit (default 100)/offset (default 0), sort_by (default started_at)/sort_order (default desc). Returns 200 {success:true, data:[runs], pagination:{total,count,limit,offset}}. Route is registered before /runs/:runId so 'all' is correctly resolved here. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to view a specific run's details so that I can investigate a sync's outcome.
expected_behavior: Selects from connector_run_history where id = :runId. Returns 200 {success:true, data:row} when found, or 404 "Run with ID '<runId>' not found" when absent. Errors return 500.

user_story: As a CMDB administrator, I want to cancel a queued or running connector run so that I can stop unwanted or stuck syncs.
expected_behavior: Returns 404 if the run does not exist, and 400 if its status is not 'queued' or 'running' (cannot cancel completed/failed/cancelled runs). Otherwise UPDATEs connector_run_history SET status='cancelled', completed_at=NOW() and returns 200 {success:true, message:'Run cancelled successfully'}. Cancelling the underlying BullMQ job is a TODO stub, so only the DB status changes.
